name: Shopify Admin GraphQL API
description: Navigable skill tree for the Shopify Admin GraphQL API. Load individual query, mutation, or type files as needed — do NOT load the entire tree.

how_to_use: 1. Start here to understand API patterns and conventions.
2. Use the indexes (_index.json) in queries/, mutations/, types/ to discover available operations.
3. Load ONLY the specific query/mutation/type .md file you need.
4. Follow 'Related Types' links in each file to load dependent type definitions.
5. Build your GraphQL query using the example skeleton and field definitions.
usage.authentication: X-Shopify-Access-Token header with Admin API access token

pagination.pattern: Relay-style cursor-based pagination using Connection/Edge types
pagination.description: All list fields return a Connection type. Use `first`/`after` for forward pagination, `last`/`before` for backward pagination.

standard_args.first: Int — Number of items to return from the beginning
standard_args.after: String — Cursor to paginate forward from
standard_args.last: Int — Number of items to return from the end
standard_args.before: String — Cursor to paginate backward from
standard_args.reverse: Boolean — Reverse the underlying list order (default: false)
standard_args.query: String — Filter query string (Shopify search syntax)

nodes: Direct access to objects (shortcut, skips edges)

pagination.example: query { products(first: 10, after: "cursor") { nodes { id title } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } } }

ids.format: Global IDs in the format gid://shopify/ResourceType/numeric_id
ids.note: Always use the full GID, not just the numeric part.

mutations.pattern: Mutations return a Payload type containing the mutated object and userErrors.
mutations.error_handling: Always query `userErrors { field message }` in mutation payloads to check for validation errors.
mutations.example: mutation { productCreate(product: {title: "New"}) { product { id } userErrors { field message } } }

cost_and_rate_limits.model: Query cost-based throttling. Each field has a cost; connections cost more.
cost_and_rate_limits.tip: Request only fields you need. Avoid deeply nested connections.
